citation.description: test_code_review_emit_retains_the_cap emits RETAIN_PER_TICKET+3 events and asserts exactly RETAIN_PER_TICKET (50) remain. test_code_review_prune_keeps_newest verifies newest-first ordering. Plan-review path covered by test_prune_bound_still_respected_after_prose_fields in tests/interfaces/store/test_sidecar_reader.py:139-160.

criteria.criterion: After 50+ reviews on one ticket, up to 50 REVIEW_RESULT sidecars are retained and a write past 50 prunes the oldest — for BOTH the plan-review and the code-review sidecar. Oracle: a unit test appending N>50 synthetic events on each path (via each module's prune) and asserting the retained count is exactly 50 and the newest are the ones kept.

citation.description: code_review/sidecar.py imports RETAIN_PER_TICKET from rebar.llm.plan_review.sidecar (line 22); no second '= 50' literal present. plan_review/sidecar.py defines RETAIN_PER_TICKET = 50 at line 48. Test at tests/unit/test_audit_retention_cap.py:50-57 asserts all equality conditions and both prune defaults.

criteria.criterion: The retention cap is a SINGLE definition, not duplicated: plan_review.sidecar defines RETAIN_PER_TICKET = 50 and code_review.sidecar obtains the SAME value by importing it (no second = <literal> in code_review). Oracle: a test asserting code_review.sidecar.RETAIN_PER_TICKET == plan_review.sidecar.RETAIN_PER_TICKET == 50, and that each module's prune defaults its keep to that constant.

citation.description: emit() calls prune(target_ticket, repo_root=repo_root) after append_event (line 147). Tested by test_code_review_emit_calls_prune (monkeypatch verifies prune is called) and test_code_review_emit_retains_the_cap (functional end-to-end) at tests/unit/test_audit_retention_cap.py:94-101 and 79-91.

criteria.criterion: code_review.sidecar.emit prunes after appending (the previously-absent path now exists). Oracle: a test that emitting more than the cap on a code-review target ticket leaves exactly 50 events.

citation.description: All acceptance-criteria tests are concrete (no skip/xfail/TODO stubs). Implementation in both sidecar files is complete and non-aspirational. Test pass/fail is out of scope per verifier rules; code and tests are fully implemented.

criteria.criterion: make lint && make typecheck && make test pass.
